text: Hello and welcome to Real Easy English. In this podcast,
zh: 大家好,欢迎收听《Real Easy English》。在这期播客中,

text: we have real conversations in easy English to help you learn. I'm Georgie.
zh: 我们会用简单的英语进行真实的对话,帮助大家学习。我是Georgie。

text: And I'm Neil. And don't forget, you can now watch a version of this podcast on our website at bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 我是Neil。别忘了,你们现在可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上观看这期播客的视频版本。

text: How are you today? I'm pretty good. How about you? Yes, I'm good.
zh: 你今天怎么样?我挺好的,你呢?是的,我也挺好。

text: It's the morning, so my brain is fresh and ready for the day.
zh: 现在是早上,所以我的大脑很清醒,准备好迎接新的一天。

text: Excellent, because we're talking today about our brains.
zh: 太好了,因为我们今天要聊聊我们的大脑。

text: We are. We're going to talk about how we keep our minds active, so kind of like exercise for our brains.
zh: 没错。我们要聊聊如何保持大脑活跃,有点像是给大脑做运动。

text: Shall we get started? Let's do it. So, Neil, do you think you have a good memory?
zh: 我们开始吧?来吧。那么,Neil,你觉得你的记忆力好吗?

text: Well, it depends. I can remember things that happened years ago that don't seem very significant at all.
zh: 这得看情况。有些多年前发生的、看起来一点都不重要的事情我都能记得。

text: And then I can struggle to remember what I did at the weekend.
zh: 但让我回忆这个周末我做了什么,却很困难。

text: end. I have the same problem. I guess that's the difference between our long -term memory, so that's things that happened a long time ago,
zh: 我也有同样的问题。我猜这就是长期记忆和短期记忆的区别吧,长期记忆是很久以前发生的事情,

text: and the short -term memory, so things that we did this morning, I also struggled to remember what I had for breakfast and things like that.
zh: 短期记忆是我们今天早上做的事情,我也很难记得自己早餐吃了什么之类的。

text: So Neil, is there a time recently where you couldn't remember something and it was annoying?
zh: 那么Neil,最近有没有哪次你想不起某件事,觉得很烦的经历?

text: Well, I did a quiz with my daughter and she's 15 and quite sharp.
zh: 有一次,我和我女儿一起做问答题,她15岁,反应很机灵。

text: And I knew all of the answers. I mean, I have better general knowledge than her, I
zh: 我知道所有的答案。我是说,我觉得我的常识比她丰富。

text: think. But she said all the answers quicker than me.
zh: 但她每次都比我先说出答案。

text: I think just the distance between her brain and her mouth is shorter than mine.
zh: 我觉得只是她大脑到嘴巴的距离比我短一些。

text: Were the answers on the tip of your tongue? The answers were on the tip of my tongue, but I just didn't get a chance to get out there.
zh: 答案是不是就在你嘴边?答案确实就在我嘴边,只是我没来得及说出来。

text: So I have a story as well, a time when I couldn't remember something.
zh: 我也有一个类似的故事,一次我想不起某件事的经历。

text: My friend was talking about something
zh: 我朋友当时在说一件事,

text: that, about an event where I was, and I didn't remember it at all.
zh: 是关于一个我也在场的事件,可我完全不记得了。

text: And she had to ask another friend to get
zh: 她不得不让另一个朋友来提供

text: evidence to prove to me that it had happened because I didn't believe her.
zh: 证据,证明这件事确实发生过,因为我根本不相信她说的。

text: And it was so strange because it was true that it happened, but I didn't remember it.
zh: 这真的很奇怪,因为这件事确实发生了,但我却完全不记得。

text: Do you think you have a quick brain? I don't actually.
zh: 你觉得你的大脑反应快吗?其实并不快。

text: I'm the kind of person that needs some time to process things,
zh: 我是那种需要一些时间来处理信息的人,

text: what people have said or yeah, I don't like it when people ask me my opinions of things on the spot.
zh: 比如别人说的话,我不喜欢别人当场问我对某件事的看法。

text: I prefer to think about things and form my opinions in my own time.
zh: 我更喜欢慢慢思考,按自己的节奏形成看法。

text: So no, I have a slow brain. Okay. What do you do to keep your mind active?
zh: 所以不,我的大脑比较慢。好吧。那你平时做些什么来保持大脑活跃呢?

text: Well, I like to study languages.
zh: 嗯,我喜欢学习语言。

text: And that's very good for the brain because you've got to remember all kinds of things and what order they're said in and what happens to the words and that kind of thing.
zh: 这对大脑很有好处,因为你得记住各种各样的东西,比如词语的顺序,词形的变化等等。

text: and I like reading. You have to use your brain to read to follow a story.
zh: 我也喜欢阅读。读故事的时候你得动脑筋去跟上情节。

text: But I don't do puzzles and crosswords and Sudoku and all of those things that other people do.
zh: 但我不做拼图、填字游戏、数独这些别人常玩的东西。

text: Maybe I should. Yeah, I do jigsaw puzzles, although I haven't done that much recently.
zh: 也许我该试试。是的,我会玩拼图,不过最近做得不多。

text: I also, So I think exercise is quite good for your brain. Is that true?
zh: 我还觉得,运动对大脑也挺有好处的,是这样吗?

text: It gets you out of the house, you get some fresh air. I feel like that's quite good for your brain.
zh: 运动能让你走出家门,呼吸新鲜空气。我觉得这对大脑挺好的。

text: What kind of things, Georgie, make your brain less effective?
zh: Georgie,有哪些事情会让你的大脑反应变差?

text: Well, my brain, as I said at the beginning, works better in the morning.
zh: 嗯,就像我一开始说的,我的大脑在早上运转得更好。

text: In the afternoon, my brain starts not working very well.
zh: 到了下午,我的大脑就开始不太好使了。

text: So I tend to do less important tasks in the afternoon.
zh: 所以我通常把不那么重要的任务留到下午做。

text: I also think that before I go to sleep,
zh: 我还发现,睡觉前

text: if I use my phone too much, too much screen time,
zh: 如果我玩手机太多,屏幕时间太长,

text: I sleep worse and then I wake up feeling a lot less functional in my brain.
zh: 我就会睡得不好,第二天醒来后大脑也会明显不太灵光。

text: What about you? Yeah, I agree. I think sleep is massively
zh: 你呢?是的,我同意。我觉得睡眠真的非常重要。

text: important. If I haven't slept well, then it's very hard to use my brain effectively the next day.
zh: 如果我没睡好,第二天大脑就很难高效运转。

text: Yeah, and that's quite common for you, unfortunately, isn't it? Hey, that's life.
zh: 是啊,不幸的是,这种情况对你来说挺常见的,是不是?嘿,这就是生活啊。

text: Okay, let's recap the language we heard during the conversation.
zh: 好了,让我们来回顾一下对话中出现的语言点。

text: We talked about our memories. Your memory is your ability to remember things.
zh: 我们谈到了记忆。你的memory(记忆力)就是你记住事情的能力。

text: Yes, and Georgie mentioned short and long -term memory.
zh: 是的,Georgie还提到了短期记忆和长期记忆。

text: Short -term memory is for things that happened recently
zh: 短期记忆指的是最近发生的事情,

text: and long -term memory is for things that happened a long time ago, even when you were a child.
zh: 长期记忆指的是很久以前发生的事情,甚至是你小时候的事。

text: We also heard sharp, which is an adjective used to describe someone who thinks quickly.
zh: 我们还听到了sharp这个形容词,用来形容一个人反应敏捷、思维敏锐。

text: We heard on the tip of your tongue,
zh: 我们还听到了on the tip of your tongue这个表达,

text: which is an expression which means that something is there that you want to say but you can't say it quickly enough.
zh: 意思是有些话就在嘴边,你很想说出来,却一时说不出来。

text: We also heard screen time, so this is the amount of time someone spends looking at their screen, so your phone, a tablet, a computer.
zh: 我们还听到了screen time,指的是一个人花在看屏幕上的时间,比如手机、平板电脑或电脑。

text: That's it for this episode of Real Easy English. Why not try the worksheet on our website to test what you've learnt?
zh: 这就是本期《Real Easy English》的全部内容。不妨去我们网站上做做练习,检验一下你学到的内容吧。

text: That's bbclearningenglish .com. See you then. Goodbye.
zh: 网址是bbclearningenglish.com。我们下次再见,再见。
